title: Happy Place
author: Emily Henry
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "Happy Place" book by Emily Henry?
Options:
A. I get more worried when I'm tired. I frown. I've been so wrapped up in myself (and/or drunk) that I didn't see how thin her face has gotten, and the pale purple circles under her eyes. Hey, I say. Are you okay? Why wouldn't I be? That's a strangely vague reply for Cleo. Because you run an entire farm, I say. And you're just one small five-foot-two woman. Her smile brightens her whole face. Yes, but you forget: my girlfriend is a five-foot-ten Scandinavian American goddess who can drink four barrels of moonshine and still win a race through the grocery store. Clee, I say.  
B. I tend to be more anxious when I'm worn out. I scowl. I've been so self-absorbed (and/or intoxicated) that I somehow didn't notice how gaunt her face has become, and the faint purple shadows beneath her eyes. Hey, I say. Are you alright? Why wouldn't I be? That's an oddly evasive response for Cleo. Because you manage a whole farm, I say. And you're just one petite five-foot-two woman. Her smile lights up her entire face. Yes, but you forget: my partner is a five-foot-ten Scandinavian American goddess who can drink four kegs of moonshine and still win a dash through the supermarket. Clee, I say.
C. I tend to feel more anxious when I'm exhausted. I grimace. I've been so self-centered (and/or intoxicated) that I didn't notice how gaunt her face has become, and the faint purple shadows under her eyes. Hey, I say. Are you alright? Why wouldn't I be? That's an oddly evasive response for Cleo. Because you manage an entire farm, I say. And you're just one petite five-foot-two woman. Her smile lights up her whole face. Yes, but you forget: my partner is a five-foot-ten Scandinavian American goddess who can drink four kegs of moonshine and still win a sprint through the supermarket. Clee, I say.
D. I always worry more when Im tired. I frown. Ive been so self-absorbed (and/or drunk) that somehow I missed the way her face has thinned, and the faint purple blots beneath her eyes. Hey, I say. Are you okay? Why wouldnt I be? Thats a weirdly evasive reply for Cleo. Because you run a whole-ass farm, I say. And you are but one dainty five-foot-two-inch woman. Her smile brightens her whole face. Yes, but you forget: my girlfriend is a five-foot-ten-inch Scandinavian American goddess who can drink four barrels of moonshine and still win a grocery store race. Clee, I say.
Answer:

Hey, I say. Are you okay? Why wouldn't I be? That's a strangely vague reply for Cleo. Because you run an entire farm, I say. And you're just one small five-foot-two woman. Her smile brightens her whole face. Yes, but you forget: my girlfriend is a five-foot-ten Scandinavian American goddess who can drink four barrels of moonshine and still win a race through the grocery store. Clee, I say.
